summary: Android AAB Exports Broken - Missing Assets

description.text:
### Tested versions

Introduced by this commit: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/4b4144cc39ee307494c89c034ea5fd180f48db42

### System information

POP OS 22.04

### Issue description

It appears the above commit changed how assetPacks/installTime is handled.  Specifically, the variable was changed from:

":assetPacks:installTime" to ":assetPacksInstallTime"

This breaks aab exports.  The assetPacks/installTime is no longer included in the exported aab.

The apk builds do not seem to be affected by this change.

Reading here: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/52310921/setting-path-in-gradle-when-to-use-slash-and-when-colon

And here: https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/multi_project_builds.html#sec:project_and_task_paths

It seems the ``:`` holds special meaning in gradle.  The fix is to simply change the variable back to using the ``:``

I fixed it in my fork. Got back home and decided to take a quick look before bed to see if the fix was simple.  Fortunately it was!  Its 11:43 PM as of me typing this though.  I'll do some additional tests tomorrow and then put in a PR if all goes well (which I expect it will).

### Steps to reproduce

1. Export an aab build
2. See that it doesn't start, can't find your assets, and can't find the godot runtime

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: EditorInspectorPlugin can't hide some array fields

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Producible in: 4.4
- other versions not tested

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable (32702e496) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL ES 3/ANGLE (Compatibility) - Intel(R) HD Graphics 520 (Intel Corporation; 30.0.101.1338) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-6300U CPU @ 2.40GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

I wanted to write an addon to hide the `Size:` and `> Resource` fields in exported arrays. This is specially useful in nested arrays of resources, otherwise it would turn into a mess real fast. But not only there's no way to access `Size:`, you can't even hide the groups in `_parse_group`. Test addon (inspector plugin):
```gdscript
@tool
extends EditorInspectorPlugin

func _can_handle(object: Object) -> bool:
    return true

func _parse_property(object, type, name, hint_type, hint_string, usage_flags, wide) -> bool:
    prints('_parse_property', object, type, name, hint_type, hint_string, usage_flags, wide)
    return false

func _parse_group(object: Object, group: String):
    prints('_parse_group', object, group)
    if group == 'Resource':
        return true
    return false

func _parse_category(object: Object, category: String) -> void:
    prints('_parse_category', object, category)
```
The result doesn't include `Size` at all, as it's not a property, a group or a category. It also doesn't show `> Resource`. It just shows the children of that which is useless for this case.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/55b87afe-ce4d-4dc9-bc65-c9647b19db60)  
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/eecda7c8-2b90-4c89-8623-4e85ea1a143f)  
The second image is shown upon opening the `Nested Res` property.

### Steps to reproduce

- open the MRP
- open the main scene (root node selected)
- in the inspector, when you open/close the resources, things will be printed. There you can see what I mean

summary: Opening tilemap from godot 3 into 4 displays blank map due to not changing tile_data/layer correctly

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4.1.stable


### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Pop!_OS 22.04 LTS on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 950 (nvidia; 515.65.01) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-2500 CPU @ 3.30GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

I know tilemaps are deprecated from 3.x to 4.x, but they still work. However, I have just started this process and no matter what I do in Godot with my tilemap scenes it came out blank. I have tried a full conversion, project only conversion, and also tried the various 'convert to tilemap layer' options.

However, I found the problem being the tilemap tscn not being converted properly. To fix it I did this:

1. duplicated the scene file, opened it and added a new tile to the tilemap
2. saved it and noticed it removed all the old tile_data replacing it with a PackedInt32Array containing just three values and it changed tile_data = ... to be layer_0/tile_data = ...

So, I then:
1. opened my backup scene copied the raw vector data in PackedInt32Array into the new one and everything works

So, whatever is in my tilemap scene is causing issues with the godot conversion as compared to other tilemaps. Hopefully this can help pinpoint the issue with the Godot 4 import of TileMap data.

Find attached sample in the attached project.

### Steps to reproduce

1. open the project and open the original tilemaps and observe all tile data is there and valid but the references to the layer is invalid.
2. open the updated tilemap and observe it works just fine, all I did was as above, pasted in the vector data



### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: Changing script class name does not update scene tree

description.text:
### Tested versions

Official 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Fedora 42

### Issue description

When a node has been added to the scene tree that is based on a script with a `class_name`, the type information does not update when the script's class name is modified.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a simple UI scene.
2. Create a script called `parent.gd` that extend `Node` and has `class_name` set as `Parent`.
3. Using the `+` button add the Parent node type as a child in the scene tree.
4. Go back into the script tab and change the script's global class name to `ParentNode` and save.
5. Hover over the node in the scene tree that was added in 3, it still shows `Type: Parent`.

The only time the type updates will be if you add a new node as a child to the node in step 3 of that refers to the script with the new global class name.  Adding a sibling node of the new type will not update the scene tree state for any existing nodes.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: `get_baked_points` does not respect bake_interval

description.text:

- Not reproducible in Godot v3.6-stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 970 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7247) - AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

`get_baked_points` returns an array of points that does not respect the bake_interval. When defining the bake interval, I expect all the baked point to have the same (+/- some small float inaccuracy delta) distance in-between, unfortunately this is not the case.

Godot v3.6-stable:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/13b2858e-8530-4e0c-ac6b-5337726338e3)

Godot v4.4.1-stable:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f80b47c-ec9a-49ac-8f2a-24ceba84e363)

Relevant code:
```gdscript
@tool
extends Path2D

const INCREMENT := 50

@export var force_update := false:
	set(value):
		queue_redraw()

func _draw():
	if not curve or curve.get_point_count() < 2:
		return

	curve.bake_interval = 0.1
	var baked_points = curve.get_baked_points()

	for idx in range(0, baked_points.size(), INCREMENT):
		var point: Vector2 = baked_points[idx]

		draw_set_transform(point, 0.0, Vector2.ONE)
		draw_circle(Vector2.ZERO, 2, Color.RED)
```

When checking the actual distance between baked points and calculating the mean and standard deviation; I get these results:

Godot v3.6-stable:
`MEAN: 0.098451, STD_DEV: 0.00018, SIZE OF BAKED POINTS: 2025`

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/904af2cd-dcce-472b-9983-9b8cf89e1ba5)

Godot v4.4.1-stable:
`MEAN: 0.19463709623233, STD_DEV: 0.0088449005702, SIZE OF BAKED POINTS: 1025`

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5794ba62-5054-4fb9-8338-7706f81ced76)

Is there any reason why the mean distance between baked points is not correct in the case of Godot v4.4.1-stable?

3. Look at the 2D view of the Path2D

summary: The scale gizmo can flip meshes inside out when scaling them up

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.3 stable and current master on Linux and Windows. 

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable (77dcf97d8) - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) - X11 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 Ti (nvidia; 570.133.07) - AMD Ryzen 9 3900X 12-Core Processor (24 Threads)

### Issue description

The scale gizmo doesn't account for rotation properly. Obviously it can't be perfect, but I think it's reasonable to expect that scaling an object up will only increase the individual scale values and scaling it down will only decrease the scale values. It seems to be an issue [with this code](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/core/math/basis.cpp#L274-L276).

Replacing that code with
```
	for (int i = 0 ; i < 3; i++) {
		if (std::signbit(dots[i]) != sign) {
			dots[i] *= -1;
		}
	}
```
seems to fix the issue, but I'm not confident that that's actually the correct choice and that there isn't something in the earlier math that should be fixed instead.

Here's an example of the issue:

https://youtu.be/fQt9rtvfg1s

Every object that's been rotated on one axis has one axis where scaling it up will invert the object. If an object has been rotated on multiple axes, it will have multiple axes that cause inversion when scaled, as well.

### Steps to reproduce

Use the scale tool to scale up any object that's been rotated. Observe that at least one axis causes the mesh to flip inside out while scaling up.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[selection.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20271421/selection.zip)

summary: Godot 4.4.1 OpenXR Causes Persistent SteamVR Tracking Issues Until Room Setup Is Redone

description.text:
### Tested versions

Working in Godot 4.4.1 stable public release, 

also tested on Godot 4.4.

### System information

Windows 11 and SteamVR latest update (as of may 17th 2025) Tested on HTC vive with valve index controllers.

EDIT: Tested on previous version of SteamVR and it works as intended. So the conflict is with the current version of SteamVR 

### Issue description

Launching my Godot project gets steamVR stuck on a "waiting" screen. And the game appears frozen. or tracks backwards (as if the headset is inside out or something strange like that) , but nothing is visible through the headset but the "Waiting" box with the tittle of the Godot project. Most concerningly it breaks other games aswell, any further attempt to run some other games (such as beat saber) results in the same bug, with the only way ive found to fix it being to reset room scale in steamvr. However apon launching the godot project again, the same issue will persist. The steam home, and the game VRChat are unaffected but other games like Beat saber end up doing the same thing until the room scale is reset. 


expected behavior: 
Godot project opening, allowing me to test the tracking ect so I may develop the project.

Actual behavior: 
Launching the project causes headset to not track, or display properly.


Notes: 
I had made a OpenXR project in Godot back when 4.4 had just dropped and it worked fine, I had no issue following the documentation and making what I wanted. However this new project didnt work at all, so I tried to go back and launch the old project and the first time it did appear to work, but ever since then, it now only works maybe 1 out of 10 times. Every other attempt causing the same issue as the new project. I tried launching it in 4.4 aswell as 4.4.1 with no noticeable difference.

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to reproduce:
1. create godot 4.4.1 project using OpenXR and XRtools plugins
2. add XRorigin, XRcontroller3d, and basic environment,
3. add script to root node to find interface "OpenXR" as demonstrated in the documentation.
4. run the project.
5. observe if the headset displays anything at all, if not observe the program on the desktop and see if its tracking headset movement at all, or if its just completely frozen. Either may occur. 
6. try to launch a different VR game or program. Steamvr home should run perfectly, but Beat saber (or assumably programs with similar framework) will not. 
7. reset the room scale in the SteamVR settings, going through process of calibrating and determining boundaries.
8. games should run perfectly normal now, however launching the godot project will recreate the issue.

summary: Add `HelperRect2D` as 2D-specific `ReferenceRect`

description.text:
Closes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12353
Also closes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/9575 in another way

Simply exposing `_edit_*` methods would bring with a lot of issues, so why not adding a node that achieves the similar way, with an interface exposed to users to customize their behavior of dragging the rect?

## Why not using `ReferenceRect`, as the latter is also used in 2D space
Even though it is, the new `HelperRect2D` is so light that it does take not so much space as `ReferenceRect`, and that users can easily customize their behavior by simply overriding `_rect_updated()`.

## What are the advantages of `HelperRect2D`?

- Lightness, as I've mentioned, this does not contain so many properties as `Control`-derived classes, i.e. the interface is neat and you will easily find the key properties of the node.
- Pureness, as this only provides a frame. Without any script, you can clip the children with this node, unlike `Sprite2D` which requires you to have its `texture` given so as to drag the border.
- Flexibility, as this is the nature of most of nodes in Godot. With a tool script, you can define your custom dragging behavior in the editor

summary: [Web] Fix `LineEdit` `select_all_on_focus` behavior when using a virtual keyboard

description.text:
Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/106536

After a bit of debugging I found that this happens because the `show_virtual_keyboard()` gets called twice. Before and after the selection happens. The first call happens when the focus is detected and the second one when the mouse/touch is released. This leads to having the LineEdit text entirely selected but the input in html5 not selected. 

This fixes the double call to `show_virtual_keyboard()` where we would only send the appropriate offsets (after the select all).

This changes the behaviour of the VK when the input is not focused. Now we only show it when the touch is released. But this change of behaviour only impacts LineEdit with select_all_on_focus enabled and is not noticeable at all (unless you keep the touch for long).

**After:**

summary: Implement method lookup for method tracks

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12436

This PR allows users to lookup method definitions by holding down Ctrl (or Command) and clicking keys in method tracks.
- The cursor is changed into a pointing hand when Ctrl (or Command) is held and hovering over a method key.
- If the method is found on the track target's script, (or base scripts) the script editor is made to jump to where the method is defined.
- If the method is a native (C++) method, the built-in help page is shown instead.
- If the method is not found, (like the `bad_method` example below) an editor warning is shown.
- The "Go to Definition" menu item is also added when right-clicking a method key, so this feature can also be used without knowing the shortcut.

summary: Shading: Refraction shader incorrectly calculates UV coordinates

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in:
- Godot 4.1 stable
- Godot 4.2 stable
- Godot 4.3 stable
- Godot 4.4 stable
- Godot 4.5 dev4

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev4 - Windows 10 (build 19044) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4bb8a084-5540-45a0-aa4f-44765415390e)

I've just been playing around with Godot's standard materials, and I noticed something odd about the way refraction is calculated. Right now, the view-space normals' xy component is added as an offset to the screen-space UV vector in order to simulate light bending due to refraction.

```glsl
// this is effectively the code you get if you convert a standardmaterial with refraction to a shadermaterial
// it has been simplified a bit, but feel free to look at what the real code looks like in the project supplied

// We calculate the normal at the fragment used for refraction...
vec3 ref_normal = /* some combination of NORMAL and NORMAL_MAP, usually */

// We offset our screen UV...
vec2 ref_ofs = SCREEN_UV - ref_normal.xy * refraction;

// Then, we sample the screen texture at the offset UV.
EMISSION += texture(screen_texture, ref_ofs).rgb * ref_amount * 
```

This seems plausible at first, but the result doesn't intuitively look quite right when rendered.

### The Problem

In real life, refractive light bending varies on the medium it passes through. Hence, I believe this refraction approximation falls apart visibly when the x and y components of the normal vector are quite large relative to the space the object takes up on screen. In other words, the behaviour of refraction for a fragment incorrectly changes with the distance of the camera from the fragment, and moreover with the viewport's size.

### Examples

These are the results observed when moving the camera in and out from some sample objects in the project I've included.

<details>
<summary>🖼️ Behaviour of three refractive cylinders with a rod in them in Godot</summary>

Close up:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f31b1973-76f4-4d7c-b816-8d000391f6f2)

A bit further away:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/505951af-6934-49f1-b875-27fb4ea5ba51)

Far away:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c9593442-9da9-4e6c-8f6c-06090957c6ee)
</details>

For reference, if we do the same thing using Blender's ray tracer (which performs more physically accurate refraction by actually bending the light rays instead of the screen-space trick we use)...

<details>
<summary>🖼️ Same scene set up in Blender's Cycles</summary>

Close up:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2514ca74-dec1-44d6-b251-2e2a9a16d54c)

A bit further away:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bcb968c3-ae52-462e-a365-546d75a69a0f)

Far away:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4c096d9f-c394-4706-830a-751d38176ea8)
</summary>
</details>

Feel free to replicate this behavior in real life with a glass of water and a fork in it.
Notice how the result is pretty stable depending on your distance to the object.

### Proposed Fix

I believe (intuitively, not rigorously) that the influence of the view-space normal vector on the UV offset may vary inverse-proportionally to the distance to the fragment. Hence, simply adding

```diff
- vec2 ref_ofs = SCREEN_UV - ref_normal.xy * refraction;
+ vec2 ref_ofs = SCREEN_UV - ref_normal.xy * FRAGCOORD.z * refraction;
```

might just fix it. Again, check out the attached project and see if it's what we expect.
Additionally, using this fix implies that the `refraction` shader parameter should have a higher value.
I'm not too sure if the value right now (which seems to be set to `0.05` by default) seems to have anything to do with the IOR of materials, but after this fix is introduced, values slightly above `1` give correct results. For example, `1.33` and `1.5` seem to give nice results. Could we make this refraction value physically accurate somehow?

**Engine Version:** `v4.5.dev4.official` [209a446e3]

### Steps to reproduce

- Create an object with a StandardMaterial3D
- Enable Alpha transparency, enable refraction, set roughness to zero.
- Observe how refraction result warps and changes with distance of camera from object.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[broken-refraction_2025-05-17_18-17-45.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20263859/broken-refraction_2025-05-17_18-17-45.zip)

summary: Jolt Physics: PhysicsServer3D spaces are never freed

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Godot v4.4.1.stable (49a5bc7b6)
- Godot v4.5-dev4

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable (49a5bc7b6) - Freedesktop SDK 24.08 (Flatpak runtime) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX (RADV NAVI31) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12700K (20 threads)

### Issue description

Physics spaces ("islands") created via `PhysicsServer3D.space_create()` are never freed when using **Jolt Physics**. This causes high memory usage aswell as high CPU usage (any physics bodies inside these spaces are still simulated).
This problem does not occur when using **GodotPhysics3D**.

Combined with the lack of a PhysicsServer3D method to deallocate/free a space, this issue means that everytime I reload my scene, my game runs a little slower and uses more memory, until performance eventually degrates to the point where it is no longer playable.

summary: Make FindInFiles globally accessible

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12418
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/98312

[2025-05-19-00.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6e069363-6942-40a0-801b-baf2cbd4ceaa)

### Summary
FindInFiles is inherently tied to the ScriptEditor as that is where the results are opened. With the current implementation you can only FindInFiles when the Script tab is open (and use the keyboard shortcut only when a file is open).

To make this more global I have left all the connection in ScriptEditor, but have exposed FindInFilesDialog as a singleton as that is the entry point for triggering the action.

In the proposal I have suggested making changes to the EditorLog as well, but that was starting to feel like scope creep and has been undone. I have opened a proposal (https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12462) specifically for it to gather more discussion instead.

### Changes
- Make ScriptEditor expose open_find_in_files_dialog to replace `_on_find_in_files_requested` to allow opening from anywhere
- Add FindInFiles to EditorNode as a keyboard shortcut, command, and project menu option
- Change all find_in_files commands/shortcuts to be editor/find_in_files

summary: Jolt Physics: Unexpectedly big performance impact of overlapping non-monitoring Area3Ds

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in versions 4.4, 4.4.1, and 4.5 dev 4

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Ubuntu 25.04 25.04 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 SUPER (nvidia; 570.133.07) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

## Issue

When using Jolt Physics, having many overlapping `Area3D` nodes will result in a heavy performance penalty, **even if `monitorable` is disabled for all of them.**

This is not the case for Godot's default Physics Engine, which seems to disable collision checks between such pairs of areas like one would probably expect.

## Cause

Looking through the implementation, I found that this is most likely caused by `JoltArea3D` setting the `motion_type` of the underlying Jolt sensor body to `kinematic` [here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/c3ae6aa10b1c0c5488cf5b5e82dd34d265c1567c/modules/jolt_physics/objects/jolt_area_3d.cpp#L73C24-L73C50) regardless of its `monitoring` state. Like also stated in [the Jolt Physics library's documentation](https://jrouwe.github.io/JoltPhysics/class_body.html#a5d8e1f66eed163868a633cdb93859f77), this appears to be very costly in cases where there are many such overlapping areas, causing collision test times to explode with an increasing number of contacts. This seems unnecessary, as the area will just ignore the computed collisions afterwards anyway. 

## Potential Fixes

### 1) Use Empty Collision Masks and Update Documentation

While I found that this issue can also be circumvented by setting the collision mask to zero, I would still argue that this is unexpected behavior that should probably be fixed, or, at the very least, documented.

### 2) Adjust the Source

As a possible improvement, I already tested modifying the source code to toggle the internal Jolt body's `motion_type` between `kinematic` and `static` based on the associated `monitoring` value, which seems to fix this issue. As far as I can tell from my experiments, my changes also do not seem to negatively impact the overall behavior of the area, i.e., non-monitoring areas can still be detected by monitoring ones and monitoring behavior is not affected at all. 

Edit: I created a PR in case this might be an acceptable fix: #106483 

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a new project. 
- Set 3D physics to Jolt.
- Create a scene with *many* (this will likely depend on the used machine; in my case, around ~250 areas placed on top of each other nearly brings the game to a hold) overlapping fully configured, i.e., having a `CollisionShape3D` child, `Area3D` instances with `monitoring` disabled.
- Run the scene and observe the frame rate/processing times via the debug monitor.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[joltarea3dperformanceissuedemo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20241897/joltarea3dperformanceissuedemo.zip)

summary: Languages are not updated in "Attach Node Script" preventing `ScriptLanguageExtension` implementations in GDScript

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1 and latest [c3ae6aa]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Single-window, 2 monitors - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7602) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8700K CPU @ 3.70GHz (12 threads)

### Issue description

As far as I can tell, there is no way to properly use `Engine.register_script_language` in GDScript despite the function being exposed. Even if you register your `ScriptLanguageExtension`, it doesn't appear in the "Language" list when adding a node script. This is because the "Attach Node Script" dialog sets up all script language options in its constructor and never refreshes them.

GDExtensions can run `Engine.register_script_language` in their `initialize` function prior to the "Attach Node Script" dialog construction, but in GDScript this is impossible. The natural method would be to use it in an `EditorPlugin` with `_enter_tree`, but since it runs after the "Attach Node Script" construction, it leaves your custom script extension unusable.

If you register in `EditorPlugin._enter_tree`, you also get this error when you change the editor theme:
```
ERROR: scene/gui/popup_menu.cpp:1776 - Index p_idx = 1 is out of bounds (items.size() = 1).
```
This likely due to the fact there are various points in the `ScriptCreateDialog` ([here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/c3ae6aa10b1c0c5488cf5b5e82dd34d265c1567c/editor/script_create_dialog.cpp#L144) and [here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/c3ae6aa10b1c0c5488cf5b5e82dd34d265c1567c/editor/script_create_dialog.cpp#L150C39-L150C40)) where it's assumed the `ScriptServer::get_language_count()` is the same as the item count in `language_menu` even though `ScriptServer` can be updated with more languages but `language_menu` isn't.

If this is the intended behavior, Engine.register_script_language should return an error stating its too late to register a scripting language.

### Steps to reproduce

Simply create a plugin with a GDScript `EditorPlugin` that calls `Engine.register_script_language` in `_enter_tree`.

Change theme settings in the Editor Settings to view the error shown above.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[Issue_CannotUseRegisterScriptLanguageInGDScript.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20238138/Issue_CannotUseRegisterScriptLanguageInGDScript.zip)

Godot v4.5.dev4 - macOS Sequoia (15.4.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M4 Pro (Apple9) - Apple M4 Pro (12 threads)

### Issue description

The WorldEnvironment background and/or Camera Environment background do not work at runtime in 4.5dev4; instead of rendering either the custom color or sky, I simply get a gray background. Assuming I set the environment background via the WorldEnvironment node, the backgrounds **are** correctly visible in-editor but not at runtime.

p.s. Godot is amazing, and I deeply <3 all the work you folks are doing. If there is anything I can do to help here, please let me know. I'm also totally willing to dive in and fix/generate a PR for this if someone will be kind enough to mentor me (perhaps point me in the right direction).

summary: Plugging in or unplugging display causes hovered editor UI element to desynchronize from mouse position.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1, 4.4, 4.3, 4.2.1, 4.2
- Not Reproducible in 4.1.4 and before.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7700 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12033.1030) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

When plugging in or unplugging a second display with Godot Editor launched (and maximised, usually), the position of the mouse when hovering over UI elements is disconnected from the actual mouse position, for instance with the hovered element being below where your actual mouse is. When clicking, the click is still processed where your mouse actually is, rather than where the hovered element is. The issue can quickly be fixed by updating the window size, for instance unmaximizing and then maximizing the window. 

If using a display such as a television (which is what I am using), when turned on or off the device is effectively treated as being plugged in or unplugged unlike a dedicated computer monitor. This isn't a large issue as it can quickly be fixed as is, however it has been persistent for me since 4.2. 

Example video showcasing the issue: 
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d8476aa9-1280-42bf-8499-3f9e2f8c501c

### Steps to reproduce

- Set up position of second display to end up in a taller overall workspace than with just the first display (Use a vertical monitor, or put the second one at a higher or lower place than the first)
- Launch Godot. Either the project browser or any project is fine. Usually tends to occur when the window is maximised, however I do think it can still happen when it's not.
- Unplug or plug in your monitor, or if you're using something like a TV as a monitor, turn it on or off. 
- The issue should now occur, and the hovered element is disconnected from mouse position. 
- Updating the size of the window should bring things back to normal. 

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

This occurs in the Godot editor inside of any project, as well as the project list/manager.

summary: New game windows render directly to the screen instead of embedding in a window

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducable in 4.4 and later
- Does not exist in 4.3 and earlier

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Steam Runtime 2 (soldier) 2 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon RX 7600 XT (radeonsi, navi33, LLVM 18.1.8, DRM 3.54, 6.6.63) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12700KF (20 threads)

### Issue description

When I have wobbly windows enabled, the main godot window gets distorted properly, however the game window has its outline properly distorted, but the image inside is forced to be a rectangle.

Expected: Window is distorted when moving.

It seems like the game image is pasted directly on top of the window, either by a transparent window or direct framebuffer write, however, since that image is now no longer part of the window, effects are not applied to it. Also, this happens both if the game window is separate from the editor and if it is embedded. 

Another side effect is that the game image isnt partially transparent when moving the window.

### Steps to reproduce

Use KDE plasma with X11
System Settings -> Workspace Behavior -> Desktop Effects -> Wobbly Windows (ON)
Start game and move the window

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
